aggregate_definition.logic: success_rate is computed as SUM(CASE WHEN outcome = 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*)
aggregate_definition.implication: Rows with outcome='partial' contribute 0 to success_rate.

current_finalize_logic.logic: _infer_outcome() currently returns only 'success' or 'failure' based on end_reason markers; blank or non-error reasons map to 'success'.
current_finalize_logic.implication: The current centralized finalize path does not itself explain historical 'partial' rows.

logic: Missing task_type triggers a segmentation gate before skill judgment.
Very low success_rate with still-medium avg_quality triggers an outcome-definition inconsistency gate.

reconstructed_explanation.why_success_is_0: Because the aggregate counts only outcome='success', and all five recovered rows are outcome='partial'.
why_the_case_is_still_not_a_hard_regress: All rows have task_type = null, so segmentation is missing.
All rows have session_id = null, so task/session provenance is missing.
The current code path explains how success_rate is computed, but not which historical writer created 'partial' for these rows.
reconstructed_explanation.most_likely_operational_reading: The 0% success signal is technically real as an aggregate, but epistemically weak as a regression claim because outcome semantics and provenance are underspecified.

minimum_intervention_now_supported.name: Outcome-definition and segmentation gate before skill judgment
scope: record task_type consistently before evaluating skill-level regression/champion signals
document or tighten how outcome maps to success vs partial vs failure
avoid skill demotion or routing/default shifts until those two conditions are met
not_supported_yet: a direct skill-level regression claim against research
a routing/default change based on the recovered signal alone
